items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: The 'Five 3's off the bench games' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Five 3's off the bench games' quiz — How many of the NBA players to make at least five three-pointers off the bench at least 20 times in their career can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Canada’s Ryan Nembhard dishes out Mavs-record 23 assists in season finale
items.blurb: Canada’s Ryan Nembhard dishes out Mavs-record 23 assists in season finale — Ryan Nembhard broke Dallas coach Jason Kidd’s club rookie record with 23 assists after No. 1 pick Cooper Flagg’s debut season ended with an ankle injury, and the Mavericks beat Chicago in a season finale.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 122, Kings 110
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Trail Blazers 122, Kings 110 — Deni Avdija recorded 25 points and 10 assists, and Donavan Clingan added 13 points and 11 rebounds as the Portland Trail Blazers defeated the Sacramento Kings 122-110.

items.headline: New-era Raptors make good on promise of playoff basketball
items.blurb: New-era Raptors make good on promise of playoff basketball — With the rebuild in the rearview mirror, the Toronto Raptors came into this season with a lofty goal: to play playoff basketball. They reached it. Next up: Cleveland. Michael Grange has the story

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Timberwolves 132, Pelicans 126
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Timberwolves 132, Pelicans 126 — Joan Beringer had 24 points and 13 rebounds on the night, Joe Ingles added 15 points with 10 assists, and the Minnesota Timberwolves took a 132-126 win over the New Orleans Pelicans.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Clippers 115, Warriors 110
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Clippers 115, Warriors 110 — Canada’s Benedict Mathurin put up 20 points, nine rebounds, and eight assists to help lead the Los Angeles Clippers to a close win over the Golden State Warriors, 115-110.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 128, Spurs 118
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Nuggets 128, Spurs 118 — Julian Strawther posted a game-high 25 points, while David Roddy racked up 15 points and 13 rebounds as the Denver Nuggets earned a 128-118 win over the San Antonio Spurs. Stephon Castle finished an assist shy of a triple-double in the loss.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Mavericks 149, Bulls 128
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Mavericks 149, Bulls 128 — Ryan Nembhard posted 15 points and an astounding 23 assists, and John Poulakidas put up a game-high 28 points as the Dallas Mavericks defeated the Chicago Bulls in a shootout, 149-128.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Lakers 131, Jazz 107
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Lakers 131, Jazz 107 — DeAndre Ayton and Rui Hachimura posted identical stat lines with 22 points and 10 rebounds each to help lead the Los Angeles Lakers past the Utah Jazz 131-107.

items.headline: NBA playoff bracket set, Game 1 schedule announced
items.blurb: NBA playoff bracket set, Game 1 schedule announced

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Rockets 132, Grizzlies 101
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Rockets 132, Grizzlies 101 — Clint Capela supplied 23 points and 13 rebounds, and Reed Shepherd pitched in with 19 points as the Houston Rockets cruised past the Memphis Grizzlies 132-101.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Suns 135, Thunder 103
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Suns 135, Thunder 103 — Jamaree Bouyea had a career-high 27 points, and first-round pick Khaman Maluach posted 18 points and 14 rebounds as the Phoenix Suns crushed the Oklahoma City Thunder 135-103.

items.headline: Western Conference Postseason Field Set
items.blurb: Western Conference Postseason Field Set — The Western Conference postseason picture is set. The 10 postseason teams are: Oklahoma City Thunder San Antonio Spurs Denver Nuggets Los Angeles Lakers Houston Rockets Minnesota Timberwolves Phoenix Suns Portland Trail Blazers LA Clippers Golden State Warriors The Suns will host the Trail Blazers in the 7-8 Play-In Tournament Game. The winner will advance

items.headline: Tre Scott, Nets Sign Contract
items.blurb: Tre Scott, Nets Sign Contract — The Brooklyn Nets have signed forward Trevon Scott to a contract. Scott, 6-foot-8 and 225 pounds, originally joined Brooklyn on a 10-day hardship contract April 2. Scott averaged 8.0 points, 5.6 rebounds, 1.8 assists, and 1.4 steals in 31.2 minutes across five appearances. The 26-year-old posted back-to-back career-high scoring efforts of 11 points against Indiana o

items.headline: How will the Raptors’ road to the playoffs help against the Cavaliers?
items.blurb: How will the Raptors’ road to the playoffs help against the Cavaliers? — Sportsnet’s Danielle Michaud and Michael Grange break down how the Toronto Raptors were able to clinch the fifth seed and what to expect in their first-round matchup with the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: Jokic plays first half vs. Spurs for award eligibility, Wemby sits out game
items.blurb: Jokic plays first half vs. Spurs for award eligibility, Wemby sits out game — Three-time MVP Nikola Jokic exited after playing the first half of the Denver Nuggets’ regular-season finale against San Antonio on Sunday night for his 65th game played to qualify for NBA awards.

items.headline: Giannis Antetokounmpo: ‘Disrespectful’ to be benched by Bucks in final weeks
items.blurb: Giannis Antetokounmpo: ‘Disrespectful’ to be benched by Bucks in final weeks — Giannis Antetokounmpo said it was “disrespectful” for Milwaukee to bench him over the final weeks of the season while he said he was healthy enough to play, a stinging rebuke of the franchise as he enters a stay-or-leave off-season.

items.headline: How will Raptors approach guarding Harden and new-look Cavaliers?
items.blurb: How will Raptors approach guarding Harden and new-look Cavaliers? — Brad Fay, Sherman Hamilton and Matt Bonner break down how the James Harden addition changes the Raptors’ game plan against the Cavaliers in the first round, and how they’ll approach the new-look Cavs in the playoffs.

items.headline: Spurs’ Fox beats first-half buzzer with pull-up three from half court
items.blurb: Spurs’ Fox beats first-half buzzer with pull-up three from half court — Watch as San Antonio Spurs’ De’Aaron Fox pulls up from near half court to hit a three-point bucket as time expires in the first half against the Denver Nuggets.

items.headline: Patrick Dumont Optimistic About Mavs' Future With 'Generational Talent' Cooper Flagg
items.blurb: Patrick Dumont Optimistic About Mavs' Future With 'Generational Talent' Cooper Flagg — Dallas Mavericks governor Patrick Dumont expressed confidence Sunday that 19-year-old Cooper Flagg gives the franchise a foundation to compete for championships, even as the franchise closes the season firmly in the lottery and faces critical decisions on basketball leadership and a new arena. "Having a generati

items.headline: Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg exits rookie-season finale with ankle sprain
items.blurb: Mavericks’ Cooper Flagg exits rookie-season finale with ankle sprain — Cooper Flagg couldn’t make it through the final game of his rookie season.

items.headline: Eastern Conference Postseason Field Set
items.blurb: Eastern Conference Postseason Field Set — The Eastern Conference postseason picture is set. The 10 postseason teams are: Detroit Pistons Boston Celtics New York Knicks Cleveland Cavaliers Toronto Raptors Atlanta Hawks Philadelphia 76ers Orlando Magic Charlotte Hornets Miami Heat The 76ers will host the Magic in the 7-8 Play-In Tournament Game. The winner will advance to the Playoffs to play the Ce

items.headline: More change coming for Bucks as Doc Rivers steps aside
items.blurb: More change coming for Bucks as Doc Rivers steps aside — The Milwaukee Bucks are going to be searching for a new head coach once again.

items.headline: Billy Donovan Plans Meeting With Bulls' Ownership To Discuss Future
items.blurb: Billy Donovan Plans Meeting With Bulls' Ownership To Discuss Future — Billy Donovan expressed uncertainty Sunday about his future with the Chicago Bulls, stating the organization has reached a "pivot point" following the dismissal of its top two basketball operations executives last week. Donovan said he plans to meet with team president and CEO Michael Reinsdorf in the coming days to determine th

items.headline: Doc Rivers will not return as Bucks coach
items.blurb: Doc Rivers will not return as Bucks coach — Rivers will not be the Bucks‘ head coach next season, per ESPN’s Shams Charania.

items.headline: Celtics’ reserves beat Orlando, send Magic into play-in game against 76ers
items.blurb: Celtics’ reserves beat Orlando, send Magic into play-in game against 76ers

items.headline: ‘Playoff bound’: Sports world reacts to Raptors clinching spot
items.blurb: ‘Playoff bound’: Sports world reacts to Raptors clinching spot — There might not have been much doubt down the stretch between the Toronto Raptors and their opponent on Sunday night, but the final buzzer still marked a major moment.

items.headline: Pistons end regular season on historic note with win over Pacers
items.blurb: Pistons end regular season on historic note with win over Pacers — In their final game of the regular season, the Detroit Pistons made their 2025-26 campaign historic no matter what happens in the playoffs.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Celtics 113, Magic 108
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Celtics 113, Magic 108 — Luka Garza put up a 27-point, 12-rebound double-double, Baylor Scheierman dropped a game-high 30 points, and the Boston Celtics took a 113-108 win over the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Cavaliers 130, Wizards 117
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Cavaliers 130, Wizards 117 — Nae’Qwan Tomlin led his team in scoring with 26 points, and Tyrese Proctor supplied 22 points and 11 rebounds as the Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Washington Wizards 130-117.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Pistons 133, Pacers 121
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Pistons 133, Pacers 121 — Paul Reed posted 26 points, and Tobias Harris added 24 points as the Detroit Pistons finished off their regular season on a high note, defeating the Indiana Pacers 133-121.

items.headline: How Barnes’ leadership helped Raptors to unlikely fifth seed
items.blurb: How Barnes’ leadership helped Raptors to unlikely fifth seed — Brad Fay, Sherman Hamilton, and Matt Bonner discuss the playoff picture in the NBA’s Eastern Conference and explain how the leadership from Toronto Raptors forward Scottie Barnes helped his team secure the fifth seed in the playoffs on the final day.

items.headline: Temple knocks down triple as potential last basket with Raptors
items.blurb: Temple knocks down triple as potential last basket with Raptors — Watch as Garrett Temple catches it in the corner and pump fakes before draining a three-pointer in the final minute against the Brooklyn Nets, potentially marking his final points as a Toronto Raptor.

items.headline: Report: Doc Rivers departing as Bucks head coach
items.blurb: Report: Doc Rivers departing as Bucks head coach — Doc Rivers will be departing as the Bucks’ bench boss following a 32-50 season that saw them miss the playoffs, ESPN’s Shams Charania reported on Sunday.

items.headline: Doc Rivers, Bucks Parting Ways
items.blurb: Doc Rivers, Bucks Parting Ways — Doc Rivers is departing the Milwaukee Bucks as head coach. Rivers is stepping down after a 32-50 season. Milwaukee will be looking for their third head coach in the last three years. In two-plus seasons on the Bucks sidelines, Rivers went 97-103. That's the Hall of Famer's only losing record over the five franchises he's coached. Rivers led Milwaukee to the playoff

items.headline: NBA Highlights (April 12)
items.blurb: NBA Highlights (April 12)

items.headline: NBA Highlights: 76ers 126, Bucks 106
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: 76ers 126, Bucks 106 — Tyrese Maxey had a team-high 21 points, and Quentin Grimes chipped in with 20 points off the bench as the Philadelphia 76ers trounced the Milwaukee Bucks 126-106.

items.headline: ‘Big relief’: Barnes on Raptors clinching playoff spot
items.blurb: ‘Big relief’: Barnes on Raptors clinching playoff spot — Toronto Raptors forward Scottie Barnes talks with Danielle Michaud about the team clinching a playoff spot and his excitement to play in front of the Toronto fans in the post-season.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Hornets 110, Knicks 96
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Hornets 110, Knicks 96 — Lonzo Ball recorded 19 points with five made threes, and Coby White supplied 19 points off the bench as the Charlotte Hornets defeated the New York Knicks 110-96 to lock up the nine-seed in the East.

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Raptors 136, Nets 101
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Raptors 136, Nets 101 — Scottie Barnes put up an 18-12-12 triple-double, RJ Barrett put up a game-high 26 points, and the Toronto Raptors secured a 136-101 win over the Brooklyn Nets.

items.headline: NBA Roundup: Hornets clinch No. 9 seed by beating Knicks
items.blurb: NBA Roundup: Hornets clinch No. 9 seed by beating Knicks

items.headline: NBA Highlights: Heat 143, Hawks 117
items.blurb: NBA Highlights: Heat 143, Hawks 117 — Bam Adebayo posted 25 points, 10 rebounds and three steals, while Jaime Jaquez added 26 points off the bench as the Miami Heat sailed to a 143-117 win over the Atlanta Hawks.

items.headline: Hawks choose first-round matchup with Knicks on season's final day
items.blurb: Hawks choose first-round matchup with Knicks on season's final day — With a win on the final day of the NBA season, the Atlanta Hawks could have clinched fifth place in the East and a first-round matchup with the Cleveland Cavaliers. Instead, they sat their starters and let fate — and the Boston Celtics — decide their playoff seed.

items.headline: Raptors beat Nets to clinch playoff berth, will face Cavaliers
items.blurb: Raptors beat Nets to clinch playoff berth, will face Cavaliers — After taking down the Brooklyn Nets 136-101 on Sunday night, the Raptors have officially avoided the play-in and secured an automatic berth to the NBA Playoffs.

items.headline: Kyle Lowry gets ovation from Sixers crowd in likely final regular season game
items.blurb: Kyle Lowry gets ovation from Sixers crowd in likely final regular season game — Watch as Raptors and NBA great Kyle Lowry checks in for what is most likely his final regular season game in Philadelphia and gets a thunderous ovation from the Sixers crowd.

items.headline: Raptors’ Quickley exits regular-season finale with hamstring tightness
items.blurb: Raptors’ Quickley exits regular-season finale with hamstring tightness

items.headline: Flory Bidunga, Jackson Shelstad Transferring To Louisville
items.blurb: Flory Bidunga, Jackson Shelstad Transferring To Louisville — Louisville has received commitments from big man Flory Bidunga and guard Jackson Shelstad to transfer for the 2026-27 college basketball season. Bidunga played for Kansas for the last two seasons. Shelstad has spent the last three seasons with Oregon. Bidunga started in 34 of 35 games for the Jayhawks as a sophomore. The 6-foot-10 center

items.headline: How To Watch Kings Vs Trail Blazers, Live Stream, TV Channel, Predictions
items.blurb: How To Watch Kings Vs Trail Blazers, Live Stream, TV Channel, Predictions — The 2025-26 NBA regular season reaches its final day this Sunday, April 12, as the Portland Trail Blazers host the Sacramento Kings at the Moda Center. items.headline: How To Watch Clippers Vs Warriors, Live Stream, TV Channel, Predictions
items.blurb: How To Watch Clippers Vs Warriors, Live Stream, TV Channel, Predictions — The 2025-26 NBA regular season reaches a boiling point this Sunday, April 12, as the LA Clippers host the Golden State Warriors at the brand-new Intuit Dome.
